d5c_e1_chaos_morning.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e1_chaos_morning.text: Final day, and you're running on fumes. High stress has been accumulating, and today of all days, everything seems to hit at once. How you handle this defines you.

d5c_e1_chaos_b.speaker: You
d5c_e1_chaos_b.text: Health inspector at the door. Angry customer on the phone. Staff member called in sick. Machines are backing up. All at the same moment. I have to prioritize right now.

choices.text: Handle the health inspector first — compliance is critical
Address the angry customer first — retention matters
Delegate — assign each issue to a team member
Take five minutes to breathe and think clearly first

d5c_e1_inspector.speaker: Health Inspector
d5c_e1_inspector.text: You prioritized the official inspection despite the chaos. Everything checks out. You handled the pressure professionally.

d5c_e1_customer.speaker: Angry Customer
d5c_e1_customer.text: I appreciate you taking my call despite being obviously busy. That actually means a lot. I'll give you another chance.

d5c_e1_delegate.speaker: Staff
d5c_e1_delegate.text: You trusted us with real responsibility, and we delivered! All three situations handled. This is what a real team looks like.

d5c_e1_breathe.speaker: You
d5c_e1_breathe.text: Five breaths. Clear mind. Prioritized calmly. Delegated two issues and handled the third myself. All resolved in under twenty minutes.

d5c_e1_result_inspector.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e1_result_inspector.text: Under crisis pressure, you correctly identified the highest-stakes issue. Regulatory compliance failure can close a business — everything else is recoverable.

d5c_e1_result_customer.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e1_result_customer.text: Customer retention under pressure is a high-value choice. Converting an angry customer during a crisis is exceptional relationship management.

d5c_e1_result_delegate.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e1_result_delegate.text: Crisis reveals true organizational strength. A leader who can delegate during chaos has built something genuinely resilient.

d5c_e1_result_breathe.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e1_result_breathe.text: Pausing under pressure is counterintuitive but powerful. Clear decisions made calmly outperform reactive decisions made frantically every time.

d5c_e2_stress_decision.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e2_stress_decision.text: The accumulated stress of the week has caught up with you physically. You're not at your best, and a major business decision cannot wait.

d5c_e2_decision_b.speaker: Business Partner
d5c_e2_decision_b.text: We need a decision today on the equipment lease renewal. It's a significant commitment. I know you're under pressure, but this can't be delayed.

choices.text: Make the decision now — business doesn't pause for burnout
Request a 24-hour extension — one day won't hurt
Delegate the decision to your most trusted staff
Be honest — tell them you're not in the right state

d5c_e2_now.speaker: Business Partner
d5c_e2_now.text: Decision made. Signed. Now please take care of yourself — your business needs you healthy.

d5c_e2_extension.speaker: Business Partner
d5c_e2_extension.text: One day is fine. A rested, clear-headed decision is better for both of us.

d5c_e2_delegate.speaker: Staff
d5c_e2_delegate.text: You're trusting me with this? I reviewed everything and made the call. I hope I made you proud.

d5c_e2_honest.speaker: Business Partner
d5c_e2_honest.text: I genuinely respect that honesty. A person who knows their limits is far more trustworthy than one who pretends everything is fine.

d5c_e2_result_now.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e2_result_now.text: Pushing through when necessary shows resilience. But make this a warning — decisions made under extreme stress carry hidden risks.

d5c_e2_result_extension.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e2_result_extension.text: Knowing your decision quality suffers under extreme stress is self-awareness. Protecting the quality of your thinking is protecting your business.

d5c_e2_result_delegate.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e2_result_delegate.text: Leaders who delegate during their own limitations build organizations that are stronger than any individual. That's true leverage.

d5c_e2_result_honest.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e2_result_honest.text: Vulnerability and honesty in business is increasingly rare and valued. The trust you built by admitting your limits is worth more than any single decision.

d5c_e3_customer_final.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e3_customer_final.text: A customer's clothes were damaged in one of your machines. This is the third complaint this week, and they're demanding compensation.

d5c_e3_damage_b.speaker: Customer
d5c_e3_damage_b.text: This is the third time this week! My clothes are ruined. What are you going to do about this?

choices.text: Apologize and offer full compensation plus a free month of service
Offer to replace the damaged clothes only
Apologize but explain it's a machine issue beyond your control
Blame the customer for improper loading

d5c_e3_compensate.speaker: Customer
d5c_e3_compensate.text: A free month of service? That's more than fair. I appreciate you making this right.

d5c_e3_replace.speaker: Customer
d5c_e3_replace.text: Replacing the clothes is the least you can do. I hope this doesn't happen again.

d5c_e3_explain.speaker: Customer
d5c_e3_explain.text: "Beyond your control"? You're the owner! This is not acceptable.

d5c_e3_blame.speaker: Customer
d5c_e3_blame.text: You're blaming me? I'm never coming back here. And I'm telling everyone about this.

d5c_e3_result_compensate.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e3_result_compensate.text: Generous service recovery during repeated failures shows genuine accountability. The cost hurts but protects your reputation.

d5c_e3_result_replace.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e3_result_replace.text: Meeting expectations is the minimum. When problems are repeated, you need to exceed expectations to restore trust.

d5c_e3_result_explain.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e3_result_explain.text: Deflecting responsibility when you're at fault destroys credibility. Customers expect owners to own problems, not explain them away.

d5c_e3_result_blame.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e3_result_blame.text: Blaming customers for your equipment failures is leadership failure. Every lost customer tells others about their experience.

d5c_r_equipment_failure.speaker: Staff
d5c_r_equipment_failure.text: Boss! Two machines just stopped working at the same time. Customers are getting frustrated.

d5c_r_equipment_b.speaker: You
d5c_r_equipment_b.text: Equipment failure on the final day. How do you handle the waiting customers?

choices.text: Apologize and offer refunds or vouchers
Ask customers to wait for repairs
Redirect them to nearby laundromats

d5c_r_equipment_refund.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_equipment_refund.text: A refund and a voucher? That's generous. Equipment breaks — you handled it well.

d5c_r_equipment_wait.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_equipment_wait.text: We've been waiting twenty minutes. Any update? This is frustrating.

d5c_r_equipment_redirect.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_equipment_redirect.text: You're sending us elsewhere? I guess that's helpful, but still annoying.

d5c_r_equipment_result_refund.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_equipment_result_refund.text: Generous recovery during problems builds loyalty. People remember how you treat them when things go wrong.

d5c_r_equipment_result_wait.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_equipment_result_wait.text: Asking people to wait without communication damages trust. Keep customers informed even when you can't fix the problem.

d5c_r_equipment_result_redirect.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_equipment_result_redirect.text: Sending customers elsewhere solves their immediate need but signals you can't serve them. Better than nothing, but not ideal.

d5c_r_staff_walkout.speaker: Staff
d5c_r_staff_walkout.text: Boss, two staff members just walked out. They said the stress this week was too much. We're shorthanded on the final day.

d5c_r_walkout_b.speaker: You
d5c_r_walkout_b.text: Staff walked out on the final day. How do you keep things running?

choices.text: Work extra hours to cover their shifts yourself
Offer overtime pay to remaining staff
Close early today

d5c_r_walkout_work.speaker: Remaining Staff
d5c_r_walkout_work.text: You're covering their shifts yourself? That's dedication. We'll push through together.

d5c_r_walkout_overtime.speaker: Remaining Staff
d5c_r_walkout_overtime.text: Overtime pay helps, but we're exhausted too. We'll do our best.

d5c_r_walkout_close.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_walkout_close.text: Closed early? I drove all the way here. Very disappointing.

d5c_r_walkout_result_work.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_walkout_result_work.text: Burning yourself out isn't sustainable, but during a crisis, leading from the front shows your commitment. Just don't make it a habit.

d5c_r_walkout_result_overtime.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_walkout_result_overtime.text: Pay alone won't solve burnout, but during a crisis, it shows you value their effort. Use the time you buy to address root causes.

d5c_r_walkout_result_close.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_walkout_result_close.text: Closing protects your team but frustrates customers. When you have to do it, communicate clearly and apologize sincerely.

d5c_r_last_minute_refund.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_last_minute_refund.text: I know you're closing soon, but my clothes aren't dry. The dryer didn't work properly. I need a refund.

d5c_r_refund_b.speaker: You
d5c_r_refund_b.text: A last-minute refund request on your final day. How do you handle it?

choices.text: Apologize and give a full refund
Offer to re-dry the clothes for free
Decline — it's closing time

d5c_r_refund_full.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_refund_full.text: Thank you for making it right. I'll still come back — I know this was an off day.

d5c_r_refund_redry.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_refund_redry.text: Re-drying them for free is fair. Thanks for staying a bit later.

d5c_r_refund_decline.speaker: Customer
d5c_r_refund_decline.text: So my clothes are still wet, and you're closing? Not coming back here.

d5c_r_refund_result_full.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_refund_result_full.text: Ending on a generous note, even after a hard week, preserves goodwill. That customer will remember how you finished.

d5c_r_refund_result_redry.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_refund_result_redry.text: Solving the problem without a refund shows resourcefulness. The customer leaves with dry clothes and respect for your effort.

d5c_r_refund_result_decline.speaker: Mentor
d5c_r_refund_result_decline.text: Ending the week by disappointing a customer leaves a sour final impression. Sometimes a small loss protects a larger reputation.

d5c_e5_recovery_lesson.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_recovery_lesson.text: Final event. You made it through the hardest week. Despite the crisis and the stress, you're still standing. This last decision defines your legacy for this chapter.

d5c_e5_lesson_b.speaker: You
d5c_e5_lesson_b.text: Looking back at this week, I realize stress was the real enemy. Not the competition, not the difficult customers, not the cash flow. What do I take from all of this?

choices.text: Commit to building better stress management systems
Hire support staff to distribute the pressure
Redefine success — profit isn't everything
Accept that high stress is the price of ambition

d5c_e5_systems.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_systems.text: The most important business system you can build is one that doesn't depend on you running at 110% all the time. That's sustainable growth.

d5c_e5_hire.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_hire.text: Investing in people to protect your capacity to lead is one of the most leveraged decisions a business owner can make.

d5c_e5_redefine.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_redefine.text: The most successful entrepreneurs define success on their own terms. You've learned something this week that many business owners never do.

d5c_e5_accept.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_accept.text: Resilience under pressure is admirable. Just make sure acceptance doesn't become resignation. The goal is a business that challenges you without consuming you.

d5c_e5_result_systems.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_result_systems.text: You survived this week and emerged with wisdom. Building systems that prevent future crises is the mark of a leader who learns. That wisdom is your real achievement.

d5c_e5_result_hire.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_result_hire.text: Protecting your capacity to lead through delegation and hiring is not weakness. It's the only way to scale beyond yourself.

d5c_e5_result_redefine.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_result_redefine.text: Knowing what truly matters, beyond profit, is the deepest lesson business can teach. You've learned it in five intense days.

d5c_e5_result_accept.speaker: Mentor
d5c_e5_result_accept.text: Some stress is inevitable. But chronic high stress is a warning, not a badge of honor. Make sure ambition isn't slowly burning you out.
